A midpoint lean may recover nominal progress without crossing the disturbed stability cliff seen at 0.06.
Slightly more cyclic abduction may restore nominal lateral progress while cross-track feedback limits drift.

A small cyclic-abduction increase may add nominal lateral displacement without the instability observed at 0.09 under the older cross-track split.
A one-thousandth nominal lean increase may improve nominal command progress while remaining inside the 0.25 m drift gate; delayed recovery is isolated by its own fixed lean.
A one-thousandth nominal lean decrease may reduce energy and drift while preserving the narrow progress gate.
A small steering differential increase may improve yaw tracking without affecting the isolated lateral command surfaces.
The 1.31 result reduced score, so test the symmetric smaller steering differential while preserving all gates.
